raw_consumer_statement: My XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX with XXXX was XXXX scamed. She went to XXXX to purchase XXXX gift cards in the amount of {$300.00} each on XXXX XXXX @ XXXX because she was told her power was going to be turned off if she didn't but that she would get the {$900.00} back after they converted her over to XXXX XXXX ( which is a beverage drink ). She used her XXXX 's XXXX credit card. I called Barclay 's bank within the hour of that occurring and explained the scam to them and my XXXX 's condition. They refused to cancel the transactions because she physically went down to XXXX and made the purchase. I think it is a disgrace that they refused to cancel the transaction and therefore they allowed this scam to play out and my XXXX is now out - {$900.00} and the scammers are ahead + {$900.00}. XXXX that? She used an XXXX credit card of all things to purchase those gift cards!! XXXX is all about the seniors and their rights and protection, seniors are their bread and butter. There was NO protection for my XXXX that day. I'm sure plenty of other seniors are using their XXXX credit cards from XXXX 's to buy these gift cards when they are scammed. Why isn't XXXX doing more to protect the seniors? Why didn't they cancel that transaction to stop that scam? Are they mandated to report elderly financial abuse?
